title: Nymphoides minima
taxonomy: Nymphoides minima is a species of flowering plant in the family Menyanthaceae. It is a type of aquatic plant commonly known as lesser water-lily or small water-lily. This plant is native to North America, where it grows in freshwater habitats. Nymphoides minima belongs to the genus Nymphoides, which includes several species of water-lilies. The exact classification of this species is as follows: Nymphoides minima (Geyler) Endl. ex R. W. Long, where Geyler is the original describer and Endl. ex R. W. Long is the more widely accepted name.

The plant's leaves and flowers have been reported to have various medicinal uses, including treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. side_effects: Nymphoides minima is a plant that is often used in traditional medicine, but it can cause side effects due to its toxicity.
